title: Cannot create column with type 'Geometry' because variants 'LineString' and 'Ring' have similar types
description:
### Company or project name
ClickHouse
### Describe what's wrong
If I try the code sample in the Geometry section of the docs - https://clickhouse.com/docs/sql-reference/data-types/geo#geometry
I get the following error:
```
Received exception:
Code: 44. DB::Exception: Cannot create column with type 'Geometry' because variants 'LineString' and 'Ring' have similar types and working with values of these types may lead to ambiguity. Consider using common single variant 'LineString' instead of these 2 variants or set setting allow_suspicious_variant_types = 1 in order to allow it. (ILLEGAL_COLUMN)
```
### Does it reproduce on the most recent release?
Yes
(ClickHouse local version 25.12.1.173 (official build).)
### How to reproduce
Run this code:
```
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS geo (geom Geometry) ENGINE = Memory();
INSERT INTO geo VALUES ((1, 2));
SELECT * FROM geo;
```
### Expected behavior
The data should be ingested into the `geo` table
### Error message and/or stacktrace
_No response_
### Additional context
_No response_
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS geo_test (id INT UNSIGNED, geom POINT);
INSERT INTO geo_test (id, geom) VALUES (1, ST_GeomFromText('POINT(1 2)'));
INSERT INTO geo_test (id, geom) VALUES (2, ST_GeomFromText('POINT(3 4)'));
SELECT id, geom FROM geo_test WHERE geom IS NOT NULL;
CREATE TABLE geo_test2 AS SELECT * FROM geo_test;
key_bug_features: Creating Geometry column with ambiguous LineString and Ring types
